title: Null reference passed to ECCCipher constructor for empty cipher
problem: When `cipher_len` is 0, `c_array` is initialized to `NULL` and not allocated. It is then passed to the `ECCCipher` parameterized constructor. Since the Java constructor throws an `IllegalArgumentException` if the `c` array is null, this will cause a pending JNI exception and failure in the native call when attempting to create an `ECCCipher` object with an empty ciphertext.

title: Buffer over-read vulnerability in HybridCipher serialization
problem: The bounds check `if (ctm_len > HYBRIDENCref_MAX_LEN)` was removed. If a hardware device or mock returns a `HybridCipher` structure with an `L1` value larger than `HYBRIDENCref_MAX_LEN` (1576 bytes), `SetByteArrayRegion` will read past the bounds of the fixed-size `ct_m` array, exposing adjacent uninitialized or sensitive native memory to the Java application.

title: Buffer over-read and potential Null reference in HybridSignature serialization
problem: The bounds check `if (sig_m_len > HYBRIDSIGref_MAX_LEN)` was removed. If `sig_m_len` exceeds `HYBRIDSIGref_MAX_LEN`, an out-of-bounds read will occur when `SetByteArrayRegion` accesses `native_sig->sig_m`. Additionally, if `sig_m_len` is exactly `0`, `sig_m_array` remains `NULL`, which will crash the Java `HybridSignature` constructor via an `IllegalArgumentException`.
